programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: All you need in the kitchen for your precision slicing and dicing of fruits and vegetables. Including three cutting inserts, a finger protector, blade holder and a free docking station.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Illusionist Eddie Clark gives Mark free tickets to his final show in the Magic Mansion before he and his partner Paul Madison go their separate ways. However, Paul plans to lay claim to all of their tricks when he goes solo, before an escapology stunt goes wrong and he dies on stage. Mark is unwilling to believe that Eddie could be guilty of murder, and decides to investigate. Guest starring Emma Samms

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A murderer bumps off the owner of the dance studio where Steve's new girlfriend works, presenting Dr Sloan with a fresh mystery to solve. Medical detective drama, starring Barry Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard takes a job as lifeguard at a swanky Nevada Hotel and lands in the middle of a family feud.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A young boy asks Perry if he can keep a stash of stolen money he found, but when his grandfather tries to return it, the man is charged with robbery - and murder.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The ranger gets involved when a vengeful group of police officers are revealed to be murdering criminals who are set free on technicalities. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio returns from Rio to lead the hunt for missing team-mate Calleigh, who has been kidnapped by a criminal duo hoping to use her crime-scene expertise to cover their tracks. Realising she will be killed as soon as her usefulness is at an end, she tries to leave behind a set of clues for would-be rescuers to find

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble asks newsman Mike Decker for help when the one-armed man he's looking for is spotted in Chicago.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A young woman is found murdered, and the evidence points to both her former boyfriend, who they discover has a penchant for violence when another of his former victims comes forward

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An experimental dairy farm comes under Walker's scrutiny when it looks like his friends at the Cherokee Federal Reservation are being put at risk. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents learn one of their former investigative assistants has been murdered, and had been selling information about how to rob a warehouse full of valuable possessions belonging to a woman convicted of fraud. As the case proceeds, Tony becomes infatuated with the new leader of another team, who apparently returns his feelings. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a port authority officer reveals Parsa's possible whereabouts as the team continues the hunt for the terrorist intent on wiping out NCIS. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is deputized then called upon to transport a man charged with a crime to the county seat.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. Gibbs has to be stopped when he tries to employ methods similar to those used on Kinah and Lala in an effort to gain information, but Vance has a trick that gives the agents an idea of what the siblings have in mind

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is forced to tackle a case with a personal element when his father is discovered drunk in a car that has a dead body in the boot. The victim is revealed to be Navy lieutenant Dean Massey, who DiNozzo Sr had threatened to kill after being cut out of a land development deal. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. After being stationed in Kabul, she became pregnant by a local man called Amir who was later killed in action - and Abby discovers he was the sole male heir of a wealthy family that owns a large tract of land in a politically sensitive part of Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. She identifies the murderer as Seal-turned-assassin Marcellus Dane, who is suspected of working for Casey Stratton - a member of black ops unit Phantom Eight. Guest starring Sarah Jane Morris

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for the cops on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. However, it's all change for the team. Last week they waved goodbye to Humphrey Goodman. No one knows how Kris Marshall's character is bowing out of the show - will he walk off into the sunset with new love Martha, or will he meet a sticky end, like his predecessor Richard Poole (Ben Miller)? What we do know is that in his place is Ardal O'Hanlon as DI Jack Mooney, who first popped up in the two-part London special. His first Caribbean case sees the reopening of a seven-year-old murder when a tourist provides an alibi for the woman convicted of the crime

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo help reorganise the seed museum owned by the descendants of famous botanist Edwin Pargeter - but it is not long before a man is found dead in the family's garden. In an effort to trace his killer, Rosemary involves colleagues from the Royal Horticultural Society at Kew Gardens, and soon the race is on to identify a batch of mysterious seeds before it falls into the wrong hands. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens a 20-year-old case when a camera and film belonging to a photographic lab assistant murdered in 1987 are found by chance in a Soho pub toilet. Peter Vaughan guest stars

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana practise their rather inadequate midwifery skills by helping Sarah deliver her baby. Graham Crowden, Stephanie Cole and Lucy Aston star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: General von Klinkerhoffen visits the vineyard where Rene and the Resistance are working, and jeopardises their scheme to return the British airmen home

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is stunned to catch Nora Batty in a church hall closet with Billy Chiselhurst. Realising he has a rival for her affections, he challenges Billy to a duel at dawn. Bill Owen, Kathy Staff and John Jardine star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can Irish detective Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal O'Hanlon stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are invited to help create an Arabian-style garden in Malaga. Before long, a local resident is found dead, and a mysterious landowner is the chief suspect. When it transpires that someone has blocked the mountainside channels on which the new garden will rely for its supply of water, it appears the precious commodity may provide a motive for the killing. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra loses her trust in the others when she discovers they have withheld information concerning her father. The revelation he committed suicide while under investigation for corruption causes her to doubt everything she took for granted, but she tries to set her feelings aside when asked to look into the death of a circus ringmaster

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of a teenager's remains halts an eco-housing project, putting Nikki and Jack under pressure to help solve the mystery so construction can continue - and leading to an unexpected romance for Nikki. Meanwhile, Leo is caught up in a dangerous hospital siege when distraught father Tom Hancock holds a ward at gunpoint, insisting his terminally ill son's life-support is switched off. Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves star, with Mark Womack and Ed Stoppard

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Laura Bryant is horrified to learn her son has been linked with a gang accused of terrorising an old man and there's further disappointment when he's brought in for questioning. Dan and Tony go speed dating and are shocked to find two female colleagues on the list. Phil's relationship with solicitor Heather Lees draws comments from Sam Nixon, while brother Steve is promised a send-off to remember

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: An act of heroism by newcomer Adam rebounds unexpectedly on George, while a quarrel at a block of flats has horrendous consequences

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The nerves set in as Sandra wakes for the first day of her new job, which sees her helping a man understand his poorly father's deteriorating condition. Craig visits the psychologist to find out if he's dyslexic, before sneaking off to a party in Bradford with Lucy. A poor showing on the rugby pitch forces Vic to consider a merger between the Scorpions and their similarly afflicted rivals, the Hoxton Giants

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Henry organises a team-building exercise in the Lake District for Goddard's staff - an adventure that nearly ends in tragedy. Jacqui continues to push Stephen away, going for a scan without even telling him, but an awkward encounter with Alison during a girls' night out changes her perspective. Drama, starring Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Finding himself in dire financial straits, Lovejoy borrows £100,000 from a loan shark using his daughter Viki's flat as security. Unfortunately the deal backfires, and he's forced to use every ounce of his ingenuity to ensure they both emerge unscathed. Frank Finlay guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer agrees to buy a 19th-century dresser for £65, and finds it is worth £20,000 - but unfortunately, he is not the only one who knows its true value and Beth is conned into parting with it. Ian McShane and Diane Parish star

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Richard Sharpe becomes a pawn in a French plan to retain control of Spain, and finds himself facing execution for murder. Determined to regain his honour, he undertakes a dangerous quest to find the only person capable of clearing his name. Adaptation of Bernard Cornwell's novel, starring Sean Bean

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Marc Warren guest stars as a man obsessed with stories of the Doctor who tries to track the Time Lord down. However, his investigations lead him to the mysterious Victor Kennedy (comedian Peter Kay), who harbours a monstrous secret. Sci-fi drama, with David Tennant and Billie Piper

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Rose travel to 2012 to see the London Olympics. However, danger lurks nearby in a seemingly normal household, as a mother desperately tries to conceal her daughter's unearthly powers. Sci-fi drama, starring David Tennant and Billie Piper

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Doctor and Rose investigate the mysterious Torchwood Tower as ghostly apparitions appear all over the world and head back to their former homes. But before long, a far deadlier threat emerges as an old enemy returns with plans to invade Earth. Sci-fi drama, starring David Tennant and Billie Piper

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Domestic disharmony ensues for the Meldrews as a routine trip to the garden centre starts a trail of turmoil including a scowling snowman, a laughing policeman and a mysterious saboteur. Christmas edition of David Renwick's comedy from 1994, starring Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Angus Deayton

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Double-length episode, originally shown at Christmas 1986. The Resistance plan to steal a plane to fly the airmen back to Britain. A parade of antique vehicles is arranged as a diversion, while Rene works on an unusual means of launching the aircraft

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd is called upon to investigate when a rugby coach's body is found stripped naked and tied to a goal post. His team's losing streak of 50 straight games has broken national records, and Shepherd is perplexed by a sense of relief in the community. Starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty and amiable sidekick Geoffrey become embroiled in a convoluted case involving a deaf birdwatcher who goes missing at the same time as a woman's body is found at the bottom of a cliff he is known to frequent. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge and Dominic Monaghan, featuring a guest appearance by David Bower

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the case of criminal Mark Johnson, who was killed in a fire at London's Union club in 1996. The original investigation was hampered by silence among witnesses and associates, but with new information suggesting the blaze was a targeted arson attack, the detectives hope they will have more success. Drama, guest starring Roy Marsden, Martin Marquez and Tom Watt

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary realises it is high time he got rid of his moth-eaten, flea-ridden, beer-soaked sofa of 25 years - leading to a trip down memory lane reliving his youth, his first day at work and his meeting with Dorothy. Tony makes an unwise purchase when he buys a snake from a man in the pub

programme.desc: Pupils from Year 6 visit the school, but it remains to be seen whether the responsibility will be too much for the crew of radio FM

programme.title: Dreigiau - Marchogion Berc
programme.desc: A fierce storm threatens Berc, and it is a race against time to get the animals of the island to live together with the dragons without fearing them

programme.title: Cefn Gwlad
programme.desc: Mari Lovgreen presents footage from past shows about Welsh men who have made their mark. Plus, home brewers who have turned their hobby into a business

programme.title: Y Sioe Fwyd
programme.desc: Culinary series combining cooking, tasting and talking all things food, with presenter Ifan Jones Evans at the helm, and chef Hywel Griffith in charge of the kitchen, creating a menu inspired by local ingredients. This time, they are joined by the broadcaster Jennifer Jones

programme.title: Gerddi Cymru
programme.desc: Aled Samuel visits the gardens at Picton Castle in Pembrokeshire and Wyndcliffe Court in Monmouthshire

programme.title: Un Teulu, Mil o Flynyddoedd, Un Fferm
programme.desc: The Joneses from Maesglasau valley near Dinas Mawddwy, north Wales, celebrate having Ty'n y Braich farm in their family since 1012, and mark 1,000 years of history in the area

programme.title: Wil ac Aeron: Taith yr Alban
programme.desc: Bro Ddyfi farmers Wil Hendreseifion and Aeron Pughe set out to realise their dream of travelling 1,500 miles around rural Scotland in a clapped-out campervan

programme.title: Ar Werth
programme.desc: Estate agent Kelly Emanuel faces the challenge of marketing a five-star guest house in Aberaeron, and helps actor Dafydd Emyr as he hopes to sell an old mill in Llanllyfni

programme.title: Cynefin
programme.desc: Heledd Cynwal explores the market town of Tregaron and meets the last of the area's drovers. Iestyn Jones visits a raised bog, and Siôn Tomos Owen visits the home of a 'swagman'

programme.title: Y Fets
programme.desc: As the surgery starts to operate under lockdown, a cat needs urgent cancer treatment, a terrier is rushed in after being run over by a car and a husky feels unwell after a diet of takeaway food

programme.title: Natur a Ni
programme.desc: A look at people's relationship with wildlife and nature, featuring Morgan Jones and his guests from the Garreglwyd Estate. Featuring bird song with Daniel Jenkins-Jones, the techniques of recording nature with Duncan Brown, and Bethan Wyn Jones reporting on June's wildflowers

programme.title: Sgwrs Dan y Lloer
programme.desc: Elin Fflur chats to Bafta-winning actor, comedian and presenter Tudur Owen in his garden, finding out about his career and the sort of person he is at home

programme.title: Codi Hwyl
programme.desc: John Pierce Jones and Dilwyn Morgan head to Utica, New York, once the centre of Welsh life in the United States where they leading the town's Fourth of July parade

programme.title: Ynys Adra
programme.desc: The stars of Deian a Loli introduce three more families' stories of life during lockdown, including a birthday party and building a climbing frame

programme.title: Olobobs
programme.desc: When giant rocks appear all over the forest, Swifty Lifty encourages Gurdy to say hello to his visiting family

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: Po and Mwnci travel to a distant valley to find a cure for Teigres' illness - but Mwnci ends up being brainwashed and turns on Po

programme.title: 3 Lle
programme.desc: Writer Eigra Lewis Roberts visits three places of personal significance - Maenofferen Chapel Vestry in Blaenau Ffestiniog, Garreg Fawr Farm in Waunfawr and her home in Dolwyddelan

programme.title: Y Sioe 2020
programme.desc: Nia Roberts and guests look back at the best of the show over the years, including highlights of one of the competitions normally held on the Tuesday - the Beef Team of 5

programme.title: Newyddion a Chwaraeon
programme.desc: Weekend news and sport

programme.title: Cymry ar Gynfas
programme.desc: Artist Billy Bagilhole attempts to paint a portrait of opera and concert singer Bryn Terfel

programme.title: Eisteddfod 2020
programme.desc: A special celebration bringing the heroes of different genres together on the same stage for one night only

programme.title: Gig Olaf Edward H Dafis
programme.desc: Coverage of rock group Edward H Dafis' last-ever performance at the Denbigh and District National Eisteddfod 2013. Presented by Lisa Gwilym

programme.title: Steddfod! Steddfod!
programme.desc: Drama about a man's plans for revenge 20 years after being wronged by an adjudicator at the National Eisteddfod in 1968

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the case of a woman executed for killing her husband in 1856, which inspired Thomas Hardy's Tess of the d'Urbervilles

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A driver whose dashcam recorded his heart attack and rescue by other motorists, and the rescuers who rushed to the aid of people swept out to sea, only to be dragged in themselves

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Catherine Southon help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the ancient skill of enamelling

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Max's health causes concern, and a girls' talk reveals various home truths, some painful

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Actor and writer George Costigan, authors Clare Mackintosh and Wendy Holden, illustrator Nick Sharratt and poet Caleb Femi take on the general-knowledge champions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Suffolk to help a couple find their dream country home with an annexe for one of their parents on a £525,000 budget, and also visits a pioneering farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holiday makers in Greece. Plus, the children's toys that may contain gender altering chemicals

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of writer and director Kwame Kwei-Armah, who has spent the last two years serving as artistic director of London's Young Vic, overseeing successful productions including Death of a Salesman, Twelfth Night, Tree and the controversial Pulitzer Prize-winning Fairview. As the Young Vic celebrates its 50th anniversary, Alan hears how a young Southall boy called Ian Roberts became the artist Kwame Kwei-Armah. He came to prominence playing paramedic Finlay Newton in the BBC drama Casualty - and his groundbreaking play Elmina's Kitchen was one of the first by a black British writer staged at the National Theatre and in the West End

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees the retired detectives investigating possibly their most difficult cold case yet. A bloodstained bust is uncovered during construction work in a cemetery and the evidence points to the murder of an alternative-medicine practitioner seven years earlier. Further clues lead to the controversial world of cryopreservation - the practice of keeping clinically dead patients frozen to extend their life cycle. Sasha is determined they don't put a foot wrong - but with the sobering possibility that two perpetrators are still at large, the detectives are forced to go out on a limb

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin explores a house in Derby, Martell Maxwell follows the progress of a mid-terrace in Mid Glamorgan, and Martin Roberts catches up on the story of an old bank in Crewe

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a paraglider hanging by a thread in a forest after his canopy collapses. A dash cam captures the moment three cars collide on a mountain road, and a bullock on the pitch nearly hits a village cricket team for six

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value. Away from the contest, Charlie also takes a trip to discover the history of the oldest cheese in Britain

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine is running out of friends, and Shannon feels out of her depth at work. Australian drama following the lives of the residents of the inner-city neighbourhood of Arcadia Towers, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Former Blue Peter presenter Zoe Salmon, medical expert Dr Sara Kayat, comedian Shazia Mirza, former Team GB runner Iwan Thomas and star of Channel 4's The Hotel Mark Jenkins set out to win a cash prize for their charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple who have a £700,000 budget to leave the capital and put down retirement roots in East Sussex. While in the county, Nicki rides the Hastings East Hill Cliff Railway, the UK's steepest funicular railway

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers remember some of their favourite exotic garden rescues, featuring spaces that invited influence from far-flung countries all over the world. They include a Zambian-inspired makeover for a couple in Watford, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a guest co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny falls ill so Miranda takes care of her in her flat, but after five days - five very long days - the shop owner begins to think about the things she regrets in life. Unfortunately, she ends up under the weather herself - and best friends Stevie, Gary and Tilly aren't exactly sympathetic. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, tennis star Heather Watson, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott and ex-West Ham United striker Dean Ashton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: In his latest discourse, the comedian takes a celebratory tour through the long-standing and essential rivalries that underpin British sport. Touching on the centuries of history that spice up Home Nations rugby and football, Jack uses the Ashes series' most brilliant and brutal moments, England's unforgettable 5-1 victory over Germany and Johnny Wilkinson's incredible Rugby World Cup performance to show that even though Brits claim to hate their old rivals, they are actually an intrinsic part of what makes sport so brilliant

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Danny Dyer, Jon Richardson, Moira Stuart and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: After Conor and Jock are almost caught by Sergeant Healy following a disastrous bike theft, Mairead tires of the duo's immature behaviour and demands change. To move matters along, she books them into a 'therapy' session in Dublin, hoping it will imbue them with a sense of responsibility. A road trip is duly arranged, leading to bizarre encounters with a bed and breakfast owner, the therapist herself (Father Ted's Pauline McLynn), and an unsuspecting pub crowd. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: Interview with the Vampire (1994)
programme.desc: A man who claims to be 200 years old tells a reporter his story of how he was bitten during an encounter with a decadent and charismatic vampire in 18th-century New Orleans. He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on seafood, cooking monkfish with mussels with Fenella and Kirsty from the Seafood Shack in Ullapool, along with athlete JJ Chalmers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team hear plans for a flat in Bow in London, a terrace in Blaengwynfi in the Afan Valley and a three bed semi in Alvaston, Derbyshire

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round, including Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Boxer Carl Frampton, journalist Victoria Fritz, dancer Wayne Sleep and TV presenter Rav Wilding take to the black chair, answering questions on The Office, the Chelsea Flower Show, theatrical producer Lilian Baylis and The Inbetweeners

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musicals version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rufus Hound, Trevor Dion Nicholas, Ria Jones, Sophie Evans, Carley Stenson, John Owen-Jones, Jodie Prenger and Denise Van Outen

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ethan entrusts Fenisha with the truth about his PTSD and the death of Big Al. Inspired by his faith in him, Ethan decides to give HEMS another try, and as the day winds down, Fenisha feels closer to Ethan than ever. Meanwhile, David struggles to manage the symptoms of his Bipolar disorder, and Robyn encourages him to step back from Matt. Elsewhere, as Faith prepares for Luka's first day of Proton Beam Therapy, Dylan's fury towards Lev builds. Starring George Rainsford, Jason Durr and Jacey Salles

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Thor: The Dark World (2013)
programme.desc: The alien thunder god returns to Earth when a powerful weapon from the dawn of time is discovered. The device attracts the attention of an old enemy of his people, who intends to use it to plunge the universe into darkness - and to stop him, Thor must form an uneasy alliance with his treacherous brother Loki. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Chris Hemsworth, Tom Hiddleston and Natalie Portman

programme.title: Behind the Candelabra (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of pianist Liberace, following his relationship with his younger lover Scott Thorson. Introduced into the flamboyant entertainer's hedonistic world at a Las Vegas show in 1977, Thorson began a tempestuous five-year love affair with Liberace, while maintaining the public illusion of simply being an employee. Directed by Steven Soderbergh and starring Michael Douglas and Matt Damon

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some the most therapeutic and poignant garden transformations, including a sensory safe space for two children with autism

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the drowning of a wife and mother in Victorian Bath, re-evaluating the chain of evidence which led to the conviction of her husband, Harry Dainton, for her murder

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of the meerkats returns from a liaison with a member of a rival family, and it soon becomes evident that she is pregnant. Meanwhile, Tosca has a short-lived change of fortune. Documentary providing insight into the daily struggles faced by a family of the animals living on the edge of the Kalahari desert. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace checks in with one of Britain's biggest biscuit factories, chatting to some of the workers via video calls to find out how they are coping during the Coronavirus pandemic. Gregg speaks to manager Nina Sparks, who he met during his visit back in 2017. Nina reassures him that all the ingredients for the biscuits are still getting through, including the flour that has been missing from many supermarket shelves due to a packaging shortage. Nina agrees to be Gregg's 'roving reporter', to show him what's changed on the production lines to meet demand, and what new safety measures have been put in place to keep the staff safe

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: The team pushes deeper into the rainforest on the tropical island of New Guinea, where cameraman Gordon Buchanan enlists the aid of local tribespeople to help him film birds of paradise performing their unusual courtship ritual. George McGavin has to contend with a giant crocodile, and Steve Backshall discovers a hidden network of caves never previously seen by humans

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day seven at the Crucible theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round, including John Higgins v Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day seven, featuring two second-round matches - Mark Williams v Stuart Bingham and Judd Trump v Yan Bingtao

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Daisy unexpectedly gives birth in the wrong burrow, but Youssarian rescues two of her pups - which are later abandoned by the family babysitters and set out to find a new home on their own

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the second day of the First Test in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. The sides last met here in this format in 2016, when current captain Joe Root scored 254 in the first innings and added another 71 in the second as England recorded an emphatic 330-run victory

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The make-up artists are tasked to create a look for UK's best-selling gay magazine, working on their male models to deliver big, bold, high-fashion looks using materials that aren't strictly cosmetic. The MUA who produces the best look will be chosen for the editorial's lead image, but if the make-up isn't good enough it won't make the cut, and the magazine won't include it. The next day at Glow Up HQ Michelle Visage makes a surprise appearance on the judging panel, as the contestants are tasked with creating a look inspired by a club culture of their choice. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of roaming this week, with Sara Pascoe, Josh Widdicombe and Benjamin Zephaniah and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Rosie is excited as the Yateses depart for their annual summer holiday, but things are not exactly as she was expecting. In 2009, Si and Em Rosie and Ben down to Devon to meet Sophie's new baby Fliss - both of whom are presented as the embodiment of perfection by Nana. Em contemplates her less-than-perfect child by comparison, and has a realisation about how far her family has come in three years. Drama, starring David Tennant and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: Real-time sitcom following the hapless Stuart as he struggles through the worst half-hour of his life. Things haven't been going well for Stu and partner April since the birth of their baby. Faced with a make-or-break date, Stu has to contend with disruptions from his inappropriate dad, extortionist brother and an almighty argument erupting next door. Starring Lee Mack, Ellie White, Patrick Baladi, Neil Fitzmaurice and Clive Russell

programme.title: Squeamish About…
programme.desc: Spoof documentary in which reporter Michael Squeamish (Matt Berry) explores various subjects - using a unique selection of archive footage and playing fast-and-loose with the facts to create a joyously surreal whistle-stop tour of Britain. He begins by offering his unique take on British entertainment. From pigeon-racing to progressive jazz, beauty contests to the Buzzcocks, Squeamish takes viewers on an alternative journey through British culture, stopping at music hall, the scene and Joy Division along the way

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day seven from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two second-round matches, including John Higgins v Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the second-round matches on day seven from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores how the invention of clockwork continues to influence modern life, using archive and three barn fixes - a centuries-old clock, a toy and a clocking-off machine

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and broadcaster Ashley John-Baptiste reveal the inspiring stories of people across the UK who lead spiritually and emotionally fulfilling lives. In this first edition, they set out to meet the people who have found inner peace, calm and equilibrium among the hustle and bustle of modern living. Kate takes to the open waters to experience how wild swimming has been a life-changer for some - and even a life-saver for others. Meanwhile, Ashley discovers how a little time living as a hermit could ease the pressures of busy lives

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the case of a woman executed for killing her husband in 1856, which inspired Thomas Hardy's Tess of the d'Urbervilles

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Daisy unexpectedly gives birth in the wrong burrow, but Youssarian rescues two of her pups - which are later abandoned by the family babysitters and set out to find a new home on their own

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees 'mad' march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: With the team's explorations of New Guinea drawing to a close, George McGavin visits an erupting volcano, where he encounters a rare bird that depends on the hot ash to survive. Back at the original site, Steve Backshall and Gordon Buchanan head deep into the crater and discover two new mammals - a giant rat and a tree-climbing marsupial

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Mark Williams v Stuart Bingham and Judd Trump v Yan Bingtao

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two second-round matches, one which draws to a close

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Two youngsters are left in charge when the rest of the group heads off to forage for food. Flower gives birth to another litter, and outcast Tosca tries to rejoin the family

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the third day of the First Test in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. England have an excellent Test record at this venue, winning 10, drawing two and losing just one of their last 13 contests since a victory for Pakistan here in 2001

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don shows how to stake dahlias, sows seeds of perennials and celebrates an abundant vegetable harvest. The programme catches up with a landscape designer in Cornwall who creates gardens that are not only sustainable but also beautiful and edible, and travels to Wales to meet a woman whose garden reflects memories of her childhood home in Jamaica. Plus, memories of 2019, when Carol Klein visited Aberglasney Gardens in Carmarthenshire to look at their stunning collection of summer flowering thalictrum and Mark Lane revealed the adaptive tools he uses for planting and weeding

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: On the final part of her journey, the actress visits Gulf of Carpentaria to see where the wealth of the 'Lucky Country' comes from. At the McArthur River Mine, she is taken aback by the enormity and scale of this operation. Miriam arrives in suburban Brisbane where she meets one of the wealthiest couples in Australia and visits their wildlife research centre, where they are working to save the koala from extinction. After eight weeks and 10,000kms, Miriam's final stop is Burleigh Heads on the Gold Coast, where she accompanies a family taking part in 'Nippers', a surf education program for young Australians

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Patsy Kensit looks back over the year in which she starred as singer Crepe Suzette in the film Absolute Beginners, while sushi, Australian lager and minuscule portions of food graced many a fridge. Neighbours first hit British TV screens, and the invasion from Down Under continued with Paul Hogan's smash-hit comedy Crocodile Dundee

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban present analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day eight from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Judd Trump v Yan Bingtao

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the second-round matches on day eight from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey reports from Bradford Royal Infirmary to find out how the pandemic is transforming the way pregnancy and birth are being dealt with by medical staff. She meets pregnant women terrified of getting the virus, women giving birth and new mums with coronavirus who have to isolate themselves from friends and family. Stacey also speaks to midwives and doctors, who reveal how their working and personal lives have changed in order to protect mothers, babies and themselves from the risk of infection

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Rupert's dream of passing on the family business to one of his children, resulting in 'the most humble day' of his life. In response to the revelations, the Murdoch Empire goes on the offensive and the alliance against him come under sustained pressure, but whistle-blowers begin to emerge, then a bombshell revelation completely changes public opinion. A sequence of disastrous events leaves Murdoch's business plans in ruins, and he closes the News of the World newspaper

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Children plan weddings for their parents or stepparents

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce clips themed on marriage, including how a porcupine found true love in a zoo, and why a sewer may be a good place to prospect for gold

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends go to Herstmonceux Castle to take part in a medieval festival

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield come to the aid of Carrie, a 10-year-old from Leeds who plans to celebrate her red hair with a gingerbread house den

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve dives with hammerhead and tiger sharks, exploring how to save them from extinction.

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Ageing silverback leader Titus faces his greatest challenge when he tries to care for a vulnerable orphan, while hot-headed youngster Marembo sets about making his presence felt in the mountains of Uganda. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares pork loin stuffed with oregano and Parma ham, served with mock mash and vignarola. She extols the virtues of spelt spaghetti, which she accompanies with an olive and anchovy sauce that can be made without cooking, and also demonstrates her recipe for sambuca kisses, a doughnut-like sweet

programme.title: The Good Cook
programme.desc: Food writer Simon Hopkinson demonstrates how to create restaurant-quality dishes at home, preparing classic chicken, Parmesan biscuits, pear salad, gravadlax and a rice pudding. He also discusses his favourite ingredients, continuing with salt and Roquefort cheese

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and chef Anna Haugh sample a luxurious dish originally created for Charles II, and explore the ingredients that went into one lavish dish at a royal banquet

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Spain on the final leg of their journey in search of Europe's finest baking. Travelling across the northern part of the country, they begin in St Sebastian, sampling delicacies including goose barnacles and pinchos - large tapas - and visiting a Basque dining club. Moving on, the duo bake dishes such as the Spanish bread pan rustico and a seafood empanada packed with locally caught fish. They also make a giant spelt sausage roll filled with chorizo, and join in with an ancient pagan ceremony in Galicia, before heading to Santiago de Compostela, where they end their 5,000-mile journey at a spectacular firework display to celebrate the 800th anniversary of St James

programme.title: The Dark Mirror (1946)
programme.desc: A doctor is murdered, and suspicion falls on his girlfriend. The police discover she has an identical twin, and each sister provides the other with an alibi. A psychiatrist is called in to determine which of the women is the killer, but he begins to fall for one of his suspects. Mystery, starring Olivia de Havilland in a dual role, alongside Lew Ayres and Thomas Mitchell

programme.title: Animals with Cameras
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan and a team of scientists learn more about various species through the use of animal bodycams. In the Atlantic, they reveal why a large number of devil rays gather near the Azores archipelago every summer. Footage of brown bears in north-east Turkey uncovers a fight between two males and a touching courtship scene, while in southern France, night-vision cameras are used to prove whether guardian dogs are an effective way of protecting sheep from wolf attacks

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the afternoon session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round, including Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: Jonathan Edwards - One Giant Leap
programme.desc: A celebration of the British athletics great's triple-jump world record, which saw him leap 18.29 metres at the 1995 World Championships in Gothenburg, Sweden

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the chateaux of the Loire Valley to the heart of the Champagne region at Reims, beginning in the city of Orleans in north-central France. Here, he follows his Bradshaw's guide to the magnificent stained-glass windows of the Cathedral of Sainte-Croix, which tell the story of the heroine of France, Joan of Arc. Later, Michael takes a spin around the track at Le Mans in a French-built car, before making his way to the capital city of Paris. East of Paris, Michael finishes this leg of his journey in style with a tour of the cellars at Domaine Pommery in Reims

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the fourth day of the First Test in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. The sides have been very evenly matched in this format over recent years, with the last two series between the nations ending in draws on English soil

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian reveals the myths and deceptions told following the United States' emergence as a superpower after the Second World War. The 1950s and early 1960s in America is remembered as a golden era of abundance, harmony and the American dream made real. Lucy Worsley reveals that to have been a carefully constructed illusion and that the era of America's supremacy was a time of government deception, racial conflict and fears of nuclear annihilation

programme.title: The Revenant (2015)
programme.desc: A frontiersman leading a hunting party through the wilderness is mauled by a bear, and a travelling companion who pledged to stay with him until help comes kills his son and leaves him for dead. Against all odds, the wounded man survives his injuries and embarks on a gruelling quest for revenge that takes him through a harsh landscape. Western, starring an Oscar-winning Leonardo DiCaprio, with Tom Hardy and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of this evening's second-round matches in Sheffield, featuring the second session on both tables, with the fixtures set to conclude tomorrow afternoon. Defending champion Mark Williams saw his hopes ended in the second round last year, losing to David Gilbert, while Judd Trump continued his progress with a 13-9 victory over Ding Junhui

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights from this afternoon's second-round matches, which saw four players return to the tables for the second session of their ties

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day seven from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two second-round matches, including John Higgins v Kurt Maflin to a conclusion. Australian maestro Neil Robertson was the first player to book his place in the quarter-finals last year, comfortably defeating 2005 champion Shaun Murphy of England 13-6

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The outing to Box Hill ends in disaster when Emma insults Miss Bates. After being berated by Knightley, she realises her behaviour was shameful and tries to make amends, but Jane refuses to speak to her. When Frank's overbearing aunt dies, the Westons expect him to propose to Emma, but his subsequent actions start a chain of events that shocks the heroine, resulting in the realisation of a truth she previously dismissed. Romola Garai and Jonny Lee Miller star in the final instalment of Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel

programme.title: What We Did on Our Holiday (2014)
programme.desc: A couple whose marriage has hit a rocky patch take a trip to Scotland with their three children, to visit the husband's father on his 75th birthday. They try to conceal their marital strife to avoid spoiling the occasion, but have to contend with the kids giving away all their secrets. Comedy drama, starring Rosamund Pike, David Tennant and Billy Connolly

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Portrait of a Lifetime
programme.desc: A programme celebrating Billy Connolly's 75th birthday and 50 years in the business, as three Scottish artists, John Byrne, Jack Vettriano and Rachel Maclean, each create a new portrait of the comedian and actor. As he sits with each artist, Billy talks about his remarkable career which has taken him from musician and stand-up to Hollywood star and national treasure

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how royal collecting has changed since the days of Queen Victoria. He also reveals how women, both queens and consorts, made their mark on the Collection, and used art to steady the ship of monarchy through an uncertain age

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: Jewellery designer Shaun Leane goes behind the scenes at an exhibition of Elizabethan and Jacobean gems at the Museum of London. The precious stones were discovered during the demolition of the city's Cheapside district at the beginning of the 20th century, and the presenter uncovers a story of forgery and murder as he sets out to find why they were buried, who they belonged to and why they remained in their hiding place for years

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: To mark 250 years since William Hogarth's death, ceramics expert Lars Tharp tries to recover a precious terracotta sculpture of the artist's pet pug Trump. The beloved animal features prominently in the painter's self-portrait, and as Lars explains, Hogarth's obsession with this most colourful of breeds played a pivotal role in his life. This canine odyssey not only examines his iconic artworks, but delves into a world of satire, salaciousness and secrets

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Thailand, from the northern city of Chiang Mai to Kanchanaburi on the River Kwai and from Bangkok to the southern beach resort of Hua Hin. Along the way, he tries his hand at the art of umbrella-making and learns about northern Thai cookery. He also stops off to help at a hospital for elephants wounded by landmines and admires the ruined temples at the former Siamese capital, Ayutthaya

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day eight from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two second-round matches - Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Judd Trump v Yan Bingtao

programme.title: Everything: The Real Thing Story
programme.desc: The rise of four working class boys from one of Liverpool's toughest neighbourhoods, who fought hardship and discrimination to become the biggest-selling black group in British pop history. With a string of hits, they dominated the international charts throughout the 1970s with iconic songs like You to Me Are Everything, Can't Get by Without You, and Can You Feel the Force. Hpwever the group's meteoric success was also tempered with personal tragedy, drug addiction and racial prejudice. Narrated by Jacob Anderson

programme.title: Beats, Bass and Bars - The Story of Grime
programme.desc: Almost 20 years on from its first beginnings, grime dominates the music charts and awards ceremonies, and even influences politics. Here, rapper Rodney P examines how grime rose from the council estates of east London to become the most important British musical movement since punk. He discovers that its success rests upon the original styles and contributions of previous generations of artists and learns that grime can only be truly understood when viewed as part of a broader social narrative and ever-evolving musical culture that goes back to the 1980s

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: A 2015 celebration of the thriving urban music scene, as BBC Radio 1Xtra offers a presentation, from hip-hop to grime. Rappers Wretch 32, Stormzy and Krept & Konan join presenters MistaJam and Sian Anderson on stage to set the Royal Albert Hall dancing to new remixes that blend classical and urban styles, with a little help from Jules Buckley and his Metropole Orkest

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: The Chronic became a huge hit and spawned even bigger LPs from Dr Dre's protege Snoop Dogg and new Death Row signee Tupac Shakur. The programme explores the hostility that was mounting across America towards the misunderstood violent influence of rap music as Interscope and Time Warner found themselves in the crosshairs of an angry political mainstream. Jimmy also discusses resisting overtures to sell Interscope's stake in Death Row

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of guitarists taken from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Includes electric performances by Carlos Santana, Mark Knopfler, Peter Green and the Edge, and acoustic material by John Martyn, John Renbourn, Bert Jansch and Paco Pena

programme.title: Beats, Bass and Bars - The Story of Grime
programme.desc: Almost 20 years on from its first beginnings, grime dominates the music charts and awards ceremonies, and even influences politics. Here, rapper Rodney P examines how grime rose from the council estates of east London to become the most important British musical movement since punk. He discovers that its success rests upon the original styles and contributions of previous generations of artists and learns that grime can only be truly understood when viewed as part of a broader social narrative and ever-evolving musical culture that goes back to the 1980s

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round. One of the best matches of this stage last year saw four-time world champion John Higgins overcome 2015 title-winner Stuart Bingham 13-11, and the 'Wizard of Wilshaw' went on to reach another final, but eventually met his match against Judd Trump

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The drone launch appears to have been successful, albeit at a terrible cost, but just as life in Brizan is beginning to return to a sense of normality, Thomas spots the cloud again. French supernatural drama, starring Gael Raes

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The authorities put Brizan into quarantine and everyone now realises that Ben and Faust have been right all along in making the connection between the cloud and the strange events that have followed its appearance. Then, Ben works out that the solution to the crisis must come from the surfers themselves. In French

programme.title: The Secret Life of Bob Monkhouse
programme.desc: The story of the comedian and game show host's life and career, told through the archive of films, TV shows, letters and memorabilia that he left behind after his death in 2003. Contributors include Ronnie Corbett, Barry Cryer, Michael Grade and Lenny Henry

programme.title: Everything: The Real Thing Story

Hpwever the group's meteoric success was also tempered with personal tragedy, drug addiction and racial prejudice. Narrated by Jacob Anderson

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at some of the records that dominated the year, featuring songs by Bon Jovi, Cyndi Lauper, Cameo, the Pretenders, the Real Thing, Billy Ocean and the Communards. Other highlights include the Housemartins, Kim Wilde, the Human League and the Cure

programme.desc: In episode four of Show Me What You're Made Of our teens they leave Cambodia to fly further east to the island of Taiwan. Here they head straight to an enormous tea plantation where they grow the tea for the British export market. It's been a tough trip for our five teens and within minutes of arriving at the tea factory one of them feels unwell. With a man down how will the other four cope with the very physical work involved? Their boss is the strictest one yet and he is not a man to be messed with. At the end of their first shift the boss proves that he is very serious about tea when he sacks one of the team. They may be shocked but can they pull together as a team to turn this situation around? Show Me What You're Made Of is presented by Stacey Dooley
