programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is excited about her new bed and can't wait to try it out. However, she's not the only one

programme.title: The Furchester Hotel
programme.desc: The Furchesters prepare for the Woof Woof Games, where three special dogs have to take on three dog challenges. However, Jasper and hurts his paw

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Pre-school live action series featuring a cast of youngsters pretending to be grown-ups in an imaginary town. Narrated by Eamonn Holmes

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble helps Grandad Tumble to water his vegetables, while Justin and his friends have fun gardening and making a scarecrow

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: There's a smelly pong in the caravan and Nick and Norman need to get to the bottom of it!

programme.title: The Baby Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas for activities to do with your little one that are all about colours!

programme.title: CBeebies A Midsummer Night's Dream
programme.desc: CBeebies contributes to the BBC's Shakespeare Festival 2016 with a special performance of the bard's magical play, tailored to pre-school audiences. Swashbuckle's Cook and Line (Joseph Elliott and Richard David-Caine) swap swabbing the deck for treading the boards as a pair of stage-hands helping William Shakespeare put on his play. Josette Simon and Roger Griffiths preside as Titania and Oberon, Queen and King of the Fairies, while Mr Tumble star Justin Fletcher clowns around as Bottom the Weaver, the star of the play-within-a-play

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Cat has lost her favourite backpack, which she has had since she was just a junior explorer, so Robert calls on someone special to help look for it

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Wufflebump guides viewers through a typical day at monster school

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: As Waffle and Gramps bake a doggy cake, Mrs Hobbs falls in love with a lost kitten

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly volunteers Mack to play the saxophone in a talent show. Mack discovers he can only play well when he is wearing a golden hat

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy goes with Kerry and her mum to the new pottery cafe, while Mummy and Tim catch the bus to the optometrist's to collect Tim's new glasses

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: When the residents of Apple Tree House find themselves surprisingly low on milk, they start to wonder where it's all going! After a bit of investigating, all clues point towards Bella, Sam and a furry little stray cat that they've found

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: The youngster and her mother play let's pretend to pass the time. Melody's imagination takes her into an animation about the story of the Emperor's New Clothes

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley visits a farm in Scotland to help pick some leaves for a summer salad. JB Gill discovers how foals and horses are looked after. Plus, how to build a bug hotel and make burgers for a barbecue

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In Vietnam, Truc spends the day in the country, in Jordan, Hamza attends Taekwondo class, while in Canada Rhys has a snowball fight with his grandfather

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Daisy and Lily take new puppy Foxy to a special puppy playground

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo finds choosing some things difficult, and that sometimes has no choice at all

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory Crawford heads to a community farm to meet some children who enjoy spending time there with all the different animals

programme.title: Monty & Co
programme.desc: Join Monty, Lulu, Eddie, Gabriella and Sam for comedy musical adventures at their family shop Monty & Co, where they mend, reinvent and recycle everything from broken clocks to musical instruments, and learn to get along together and celebrate their differences

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble goes to Chef Tumble's restaurant where everything is either too big or too small, while Justin and his friends visit a model village

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: A journey back in time to see how dinosaurs lived, utilising the latest digital CGI and green-screen technology. In the first edition, presenter Andy Day accidentally breaks a piece of pumice stone at the museum, so he tries to get some more by travelling back in time to the age of the Tyrannosaurus Rex

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie finds out how a snail moves and discovers how a tractor is made in a factory.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Jemima is short on fruit ingredients for her flapjacks, and in a misunderstanding with Peter, she heads to Mr McGregor's garden to find some

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran busts a groove when she teaches JoJo the moves to a traditional Saint Lucian dance. JoJo finds that learning a dance can take time, until she realises she can put her own twist on it.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Doug, Jess and Gramps take Waffle to his first puppy training class

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan visits a farm as workers prepare their pigs for a visit from the vet. Rory Crawford helps a vet with an examination on some amazing alpacas

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble
programme.desc: Join Mr Tumble at home today, where he has spent most of his time making a ‘Wash Your Hands' poster! The Makaton word of the day is 'wash your hands', and Yala and Charlie have sent Mr Tumble a very fishy joke!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Crocodile chases Monkey up a tree when he discovers he has been tricked

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day jets off to Africa's Sahara Desert in search of a naked mole rat. His mission takes him into its underground burrow

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Alice organises a relaxation class, and Molly helps get people to attend. However, just as everyone starts to relax, Bob and Moira start on some very noisy work

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The youngster realises the differences between her two grandmothers, and ends up following a mystery map that shows her where to find an unexpected kind of treasure

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: When a fairground is held in the park, Sarah and Duck struggle to find their bench

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The duo head for the zoo where Lola wants to buy a toy seal, but she spends all her pocket money before reaching its shop

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee is heartbroken when he can't find his favourite toy, Action Ape, and goes straight to Mo for help. Mo is more interested in helping Tee learn to look for things. Where did you last see him, Tee?

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny hears a strange voice joining in with her singing and discovers a flower singing as she travels through space on a rocky asteroid. Tiny discovers that the flower is lonely, and comes up with a clever plan to help her

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines go to meet the Wottingers, but they are out

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the case of a woman executed for killing her husband in 1856, which inspired Thomas Hardy's Tess of the d'Urbervilles

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A driver whose dashcam recorded his heart attack and rescue by other motorists, and the rescuers who rushed to the aid of people swept out to sea, only to be dragged in themselves

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Catherine Southon help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the ancient skill of enamelling

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Max's health causes concern, and a girls' talk reveals various home truths, some painful

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Actor and writer George Costigan, authors Clare Mackintosh and Wendy Holden, illustrator Nick Sharratt and poet Caleb Femi take on the general-knowledge champions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Suffolk to help a couple find their dream country home with an annexe for one of their parents on a £525,000 budget, and also visits a pioneering farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tracks wildlife in one of Wales' extraordinary landscapes - the Cambrian Mountains. In this first edition, he seeks out pine marten and ospreys

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of writer and director Kwame Kwei-Armah, who has spent the last two years serving as artistic director of London's Young Vic, overseeing successful productions including Death of a Salesman, Twelfth Night, Tree and the controversial Pulitzer Prize-winning Fairview. As the Young Vic celebrates its 50th anniversary, Alan hears how a young Southall boy called Ian Roberts became the artist Kwame Kwei-Armah. He came to prominence playing paramedic Finlay Newton in the BBC drama Casualty - and his groundbreaking play Elmina's Kitchen was one of the first by a black British writer staged at the National Theatre and in the West End

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees the retired detectives investigating possibly their most difficult cold case yet. A bloodstained bust is uncovered during construction work in a cemetery and the evidence points to the murder of an alternative-medicine practitioner seven years earlier. Further clues lead to the controversial world of cryopreservation - the practice of keeping clinically dead patients frozen to extend their life cycle. Sasha is determined they don't put a foot wrong - but with the sobering possibility that two perpetrators are still at large, the detectives are forced to go out on a limb

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin explores a house in Derby, Martell Maxwell follows the progress of a mid-terrace in Mid Glamorgan, and Martin Roberts catches up on the story of an old bank in Crewe

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a paraglider hanging by a thread in a forest after his canopy collapses. A dash cam captures the moment three cars collide on a mountain road, and a bullock on the pitch nearly hits a village cricket team for six

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value. Away from the contest, Charlie also takes a trip to discover the history of the oldest cheese in Britain

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine is running out of friends, and Shannon feels out of her depth at work. Australian drama following the lives of the residents of the inner-city neighbourhood of Arcadia Towers, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Former Blue Peter presenter Zoe Salmon, medical expert Dr Sara Kayat, comedian Shazia Mirza, former Team GB runner Iwan Thomas and star of Channel 4's The Hotel Mark Jenkins set out to win a cash prize for their charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple who have a £700,000 budget to leave the capital and put down retirement roots in East Sussex. While in the county, Nicki rides the Hastings East Hill Cliff Railway, the UK's steepest funicular railway

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers remember some of their favourite exotic garden rescues, featuring spaces that invited influence from far-flung countries all over the world. They include a Zambian-inspired makeover for a couple in Watford, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a guest co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny falls ill so Miranda takes care of her in her flat, but after five days - five very long days - the shop owner begins to think about the things she regrets in life. Unfortunately, she ends up under the weather herself - and best friends Stevie, Gary and Tilly aren't exactly sympathetic. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, tennis star Heather Watson, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott and ex-West Ham United striker Dean Ashton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen embarks on a quest to discover the truth about the human body. It's a story of breakdancers, contortionists and '80s jacuzzis, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: In his latest discourse, the comedian takes a celebratory tour through the long-standing and essential rivalries that underpin British sport. Touching on the centuries of history that spice up Home Nations rugby and football, Jack uses the Ashes series' most brilliant and brutal moments, England's unforgettable 5-1 victory over Germany and Johnny Wilkinson's incredible Rugby World Cup performance to show that even though Brits claim to hate their old rivals, they are actually an intrinsic part of what makes sport so brilliant

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian gets a job as a tattooist's apprentice, but discovers it could be the most painful job of all when he is told he will have to go under the needle himself

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: After Conor and Jock are almost caught by Sergeant Healy following a disastrous bike theft, Mairead tires of the duo's immature behaviour and demands change. To move matters along, she books them into a 'therapy' session in Dublin, hoping it will imbue them with a sense of responsibility. A road trip is duly arranged, leading to bizarre encounters with a bed and breakfast owner, the therapist herself (Father Ted's Pauline McLynn), and an unsuspecting pub crowd. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: Interview with the Vampire (1994)
programme.desc: A man who claims to be 200 years old tells a reporter his story of how he was bitten during an encounter with a decadent and charismatic vampire in 18th-century New Orleans. He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on seafood, cooking monkfish with mussels with Fenella and Kirsty from the Seafood Shack in Ullapool, along with athlete JJ Chalmers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team hear plans for a flat in Bow in London, a terrace in Blaengwynfi in the Afan Valley and a three bed semi in Alvaston, Derbyshire

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round, including Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Boxer Carl Frampton, journalist Victoria Fritz, dancer Wayne Sleep and TV presenter Rav Wilding take to the black chair, answering questions on The Office, the Chelsea Flower Show, theatrical producer Lilian Baylis and The Inbetweeners

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musicals version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rufus Hound, Trevor Dion Nicholas, Ria Jones, Sophie Evans, Carley Stenson, John Owen-Jones, Jodie Prenger and Denise Van Outen

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ethan entrusts Fenisha with the truth about his PTSD and the death of Big Al. Inspired by his faith in him, Ethan decides to give HEMS another try, and as the day winds down, Fenisha feels closer to Ethan than ever. Meanwhile, David struggles to manage the symptoms of his Bipolar disorder, and Robyn encourages him to step back from Matt. Elsewhere, as Faith prepares for Luka's first day of Proton Beam Therapy, Dylan's fury towards Lev builds. Starring George Rainsford, Jason Durr and Jacey Salles

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Thor: The Dark World (2013)
programme.desc: The alien thunder god returns to Earth when a powerful weapon from the dawn of time is discovered. The device attracts the attention of an old enemy of his people, who intends to use it to plunge the universe into darkness - and to stop him, Thor must form an uneasy alliance with his treacherous brother Loki. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Chris Hemsworth, Tom Hiddleston and Natalie Portman

programme.title: Behind the Candelabra (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of pianist Liberace, following his relationship with his younger lover Scott Thorson. Introduced into the flamboyant entertainer's hedonistic world at a Las Vegas show in 1977, Thorson began a tempestuous five-year love affair with Liberace, while maintaining the public illusion of simply being an employee. Directed by Steven Soderbergh and starring Michael Douglas and Matt Damon

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, tennis star Heather Watson, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott and ex-West Ham United striker Dean Ashton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: In his latest discourse, the comedian takes a celebratory tour through the long-standing and essential rivalries that underpin British sport. Touching on the centuries of history that spice up Home Nations rugby and football, Jack uses the Ashes series' most brilliant and brutal moments, England's unforgettable 5-1 victory over Germany and Johnny Wilkinson's incredible Rugby World Cup performance to show that even though Brits claim to hate their old rivals, they are actually an intrinsic part of what makes sport so brilliant

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Danny Dyer, Jon Richardson, Moira Stuart and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: After Conor and Jock are almost caught by Sergeant Healy following a disastrous bike theft, Mairead tires of the duo's immature behaviour and demands change. To move matters along, she books them into a 'therapy' session in Dublin, hoping it will imbue them with a sense of responsibility. A road trip is duly arranged, leading to bizarre encounters with a bed and breakfast owner, the therapist herself (Father Ted's Pauline McLynn), and an unsuspecting pub crowd. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: Interview with the Vampire (1994)
programme.desc: A man who claims to be 200 years old tells a reporter his story of how he was bitten during an encounter with a decadent and charismatic vampire in 18th-century New Orleans. He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

programme.desc: Jon Kay and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ethan entrusts Fenisha with the truth about his PTSD and the death of Big Al. Inspired by his faith in him, Ethan decides to give HEMS another try, and as the day winds down, Fenisha feels closer to Ethan than ever. Meanwhile, David struggles to manage the symptoms of his Bipolar disorder, and Robyn encourages him to step back from Matt. Elsewhere, as Faith prepares for Luka's first day of Proton Beam Therapy, Dylan's fury towards Lev builds. Starring George Rainsford, Jason Durr and Jacey Salles

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Thor: The Dark World (2013)
programme.desc: The alien thunder god returns to Earth when a powerful weapon from the dawn of time is discovered. The device attracts the attention of an old enemy of his people, who intends to use it to plunge the universe into darkness - and to stop him, Thor must form an uneasy alliance with his treacherous brother Loki. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Chris Hemsworth, Tom Hiddleston and Natalie Portman

programme.title: Behind the Candelabra (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of pianist Liberace, following his relationship with his younger lover Scott Thorson. Introduced into the flamboyant entertainer's hedonistic world at a Las Vegas show in 1977, Thorson began a tempestuous five-year love affair with Liberace, while maintaining the public illusion of simply being an employee. Directed by Steven Soderbergh and starring Michael Douglas and Matt Damon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the case of a woman executed for killing her husband in 1856, which inspired Thomas Hardy's Tess of the d'Urbervilles

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A driver whose dashcam recorded his heart attack and rescue by other motorists, and the rescuers who rushed to the aid of people swept out to sea, only to be dragged in themselves

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Catherine Southon help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the ancient skill of enamelling

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Max's health causes concern, and a girls' talk reveals various home truths, some painful

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Actor and writer George Costigan, authors Clare Mackintosh and Wendy Holden, illustrator Nick Sharratt and poet Caleb Femi take on the general-knowledge champions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Suffolk to help a couple find their dream country home with an annexe for one of their parents on a £525,000 budget, and also visits a pioneering farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Home Cook Heroes
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring a variety of recipes. In this first edition, Suzie uses a rump steak to create enchiladas with homemade tortilla wraps, and sticky toffee pudding

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Belfast Harbour: Cruises, Cranes & Cargo
programme.desc: The container terminal finally welcomes their new semi-automated cranes, H&W workers discover their fate and Belfast Harbour Police are on patrol as Halloween descends on Belfast

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of writer and director Kwame Kwei-Armah, who has spent the last two years serving as artistic director of London's Young Vic, overseeing successful productions including Death of a Salesman, Twelfth Night, Tree and the controversial Pulitzer Prize-winning Fairview. As the Young Vic celebrates its 50th anniversary, Alan hears how a young Southall boy called Ian Roberts became the artist Kwame Kwei-Armah. He came to prominence playing paramedic Finlay Newton in the BBC drama Casualty - and his groundbreaking play Elmina's Kitchen was one of the first by a black British writer staged at the National Theatre and in the West End

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees the retired detectives investigating possibly their most difficult cold case yet. A bloodstained bust is uncovered during construction work in a cemetery and the evidence points to the murder of an alternative-medicine practitioner seven years earlier. Further clues lead to the controversial world of cryopreservation - the practice of keeping clinically dead patients frozen to extend their life cycle. Sasha is determined they don't put a foot wrong - but with the sobering possibility that two perpetrators are still at large, the detectives are forced to go out on a limb

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin explores a house in Derby, Martell Maxwell follows the progress of a mid-terrace in Mid Glamorgan, and Martin Roberts catches up on the story of an old bank in Crewe

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a paraglider hanging by a thread in a forest after his canopy collapses. A dash cam captures the moment three cars collide on a mountain road, and a bullock on the pitch nearly hits a village cricket team for six

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value. Away from the contest, Charlie also takes a trip to discover the history of the oldest cheese in Britain

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine is running out of friends, and Shannon feels out of her depth at work. Australian drama following the lives of the residents of the inner-city neighbourhood of Arcadia Towers, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Former Blue Peter presenter Zoe Salmon, medical expert Dr Sara Kayat, comedian Shazia Mirza, former Team GB runner Iwan Thomas and star of Channel 4's The Hotel Mark Jenkins set out to win a cash prize for their charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple who have a £700,000 budget to leave the capital and put down retirement roots in East Sussex. While in the county, Nicki rides the Hastings East Hill Cliff Railway, the UK's steepest funicular railway

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers remember some of their favourite exotic garden rescues, featuring spaces that invited influence from far-flung countries all over the world. They include a Zambian-inspired makeover for a couple in Watford, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the rolling Cotswold hills in their 1970s off-roader. There are ancient artefacts, dry stone walls and lots and lots of sheep - but who will win out at the final auction in Devizes, Wiltshire?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.desc: Alex Jones and a guest co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny falls ill so Miranda takes care of her in her flat, but after five days - five very long days - the shop owner begins to think about the things she regrets in life. programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Some are predicting that despite the easing of the lockdown, the arts will be devastated by the financial impact of the pandemic. What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Some are predicting that despite the easing of the lockdown, the arts will be devastated by the financial impact of the pandemic. What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In times of crisis, we learn about who we really are, and so it is that the global coronavirus pandemic is revealing truths about humankind and how we balance self and collective interest. Stephen Sackur speaks to writer and historian Rutger Bregman whose book Humankind: A Hopeful History is making waves around the world. Do we humans massively underestimate our capacity to change things for the better? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Wednesday 27 May.

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)
programme.desc: Desperate to be a true vampire, Resus makes a wish at a wishing well. However, his desire comes at a great cost

programme.title: Naidheachdan Neonach CBBC ALBA
programme.desc: Ellen NicDhòmhnaill introduces her personal highlights from the craziest news show on BBC ALBA

programme.title: Binneas

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: A mix of archive and new performances from previous headliners and emerging talent. Presented by Niall Iain Macdonald and Fiona Mackenzie

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: The whole country is in lockdown and Sweeny is working out how to keep the croft going during these challenging times. With no camera crew permitted to visit for now, he is also getting to grips with self-shooting

programme.title: Ceol Duthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean visits Spar Cave on the Isle of Skye, finding that even reaching his destination proves tricky

programme.title: Manran aig Barrowland
programme.desc: A concert from Mànran & Trail West recorded on St Andrew's Day, 2018. The bands play in front of the 2,000 music fans who packed this concert at the fabled Barrowland Ballroom

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: The schoolchildren write articles for the local newspaper and Kate Morag realises trying to find an interesting story is a difficult task

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: Peekaboo leaves a colourful trail that Witzy has lots of fun following

programme.title: Lon le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod and her friends Alexia and Scarlett make delicious cheese and oat scones. However, before starting they need to make sure they have all the ingredients

programme.title: Bruno
programme.desc: The inquisitive monkey cleans up his toys with the help of the Little Red Wagon

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Oona is forever in a hurry to learn and get things done but Papa shows her that slow and steady is often the best way of doing something

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: The lighthouse needs a new bulb - an important and fragile delivery that falls to the long-serving mailman

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When Heather and Windshear land on Dragon's Edge to join the Dragon Rider crew, they quickly discover that Windshear does not play well with others

programme.desc: Fearchar tries his hand at clay pigeon shooting

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Peter the inventor is sent on a journey after the locals notice his idleness

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: Avril Irvine looks after the helicopter flights at Sumburgh, when an unusual thick fog envelops the airport and the rigs offshore - and plays havoc with the schedules

programme.title: Ceol Duthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Eadar Chluich

programme.title: Port

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Former Strictly stars Jay McGuiness and Kimberley Walsh talk about appearing in Sleepless - one of the first musicals to open since lockdown began

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the dealers are in Warwick, where Stewart Hofgartner makes his oddest purchase yet, and Fay Rutter and Henry Nicholls both bid for paintings

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A family from Bognor Regis answers questions based on top 10 lists, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to win the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. This time, a workman is crushed by a falling building, a woman loses her wig on a rollercoaster and a parachutist is knocked unconscious by the wing of a plane

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh presents this consumer show in which she and a team of experts give families an eight-week challenge to get fitter, eat better and save money on their shopping. This week, Ranvir and her team of experts are in Southport with the Paiva family, helping mum Fatima to ditch diet pills and quick fixes for long-term healthy living

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher continues to explore the 870-mile Wales Coast Path. This time around, he travels down Cardigan Bay, where he helps monitor the local dolphin population, discovers a 4,000-year-old sunken forest on a beach and takes part in a Celtic Longboat rowing race

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three more factories producing some of the nation's favourite food-and-drink items. It reveals how millions of Drumstick lollipops are made at the Swizzels sweet factory in Derbyshire, and how the Mackie family produces its famous ice-cream in Aberdeen. There is also a look behind the scenes at the Robinsons squash factory, where orange cordial destined for the Centre Court of Wimbledon is produced. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: The establishment hosts a dinner dance, John Williams launches a new ingredient onto the menu and some Spanish superstars arrive to prepare for Prince Charles's 70th birthday party. The programme also shows how the hotel is turned around overnight for Christmas, with a 25ft Christmas tree beig installed and the kitchen staff preparing up to 500 Michelin-star standard turkey roast dinners a week

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unforgettable RWC Moments
programme.desc: A look back at classic action from the Rugby World Cup, featuring examples of outstanding individual performances from the likes of Serge Blanco, Jonah Lomu, and Jonny Wilkinson, plus memorable team triumphs such as South Africa's tournament win on home soil in 1995

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five bed specialists answer questions on subjects including Blue Peter, capital cities and Robert De Niro films, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Staffordshire County Showground plays host to David Dickinson and his dealers. A pair of horse prints have Karen Dalmeny in tears, Stewart Hofgartner goes 'pirate crazy' for a porthole, while Mark Stevens gets hot under the collar when some saucy films come into the den

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A family of five from the north-east of England answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy and Nate have a difficult conversation, Nicola feels for Gabby, and Sam lets slip private information about Lydia

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff swears that Elaine is peddling lies, she's not Tim's Mum and Alya must have put her up to it in a bid to drive a wedge between them. Leanne urges Toyah to set the fostering wheels in motion as she deserves some happiness. And Adam seethes when Sarah fails to mention Gary's change of heart over the rent

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of a young man's body in an area notorious for illicit teenage gatherings, the corpse wrapped in plastic sheeting and having received a blow to the head. Vera follows the trail to a funfair where the victim used to work and a church where he sought refuge, having recently been made homeless. She delves into his family history to discover why he was on the streets when his parents lived nearby, while forensic evidence implicates the son of the vicar, who has a criminal past

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Danish Girl (2015)
programme.desc: Artist Gerda Wegener unveils a portrait of her husband Einar dressed as a woman in 1920s Copenhagen. Einar realises that the woman in the painting is his true identity, and decides to become one of the first people to undergo sex reassignment surgery. Period drama based on David Ebershoff's novel inspired by the true story of artist Lili Elbe, starring Eddie Redmayne and an Oscar-winning Alicia Vikander

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: Dad tries to get Dana up and ready for school, but she is busy dreaming about discovering the first T Rex fossil

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he's hunting down a rat in his attic, Magoo finds himself on the theme park, devoted to... the glory of Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo comes across Fizz encased in ice and thinks he has found a prehistoric man. He thaws him out and acts like a caveman, so as not to distress him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's helping out at an understaffed veterinary clinic. In fact, he's at the supermarket, where Fizz is trying to sell his cleaning products.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Kayo rides aboard Fireflash, a Brains-invented supersonic airliner, but is unaware its control has been seized by a mystery individual. Meanwhile, International Rescue realises the plane has vanished from its monitors and tries to locate it. After noticing the aircraft is flying in the wrong direction, Kayo manages to avoid a powerful gas that has knocked out both the passengers and crew

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tests out Fizz's latest invention, a three-piece swimsuit to make beachgoers look chic. Magoo mistakes him for the President.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz wants to cure Magoo's near-sightedness for good. He and Weasel go over to his house, only to be taken for Magoo's parents on a surprise visit.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced to spend the day outside in his doghouse due to Grandma Mikkelsen's fur allergy

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is ecstatic to learn his favourite singer is performing at the London Palladium and moves heaven and Earth to get her to sign his autograph book. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Zombozo hypnotizes Grandpa Max's entire baking class

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Wet Wet Wet singer Marti Pellow, chefs Brian Turner and Cherish Finden, and seafood expert Dan the Fish Man

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a menu perfect for enjoying inside and out. Dishes featured include spiced beer can chicken with saffron potatoes, homemade crisps and dips, summer cocktails, gammon steak, celeriac remoulade and coconut and polenta cake with honey syrup

programme.title: Twins (1988)
programme.desc: An unlikely pair of brothers, who were born as the result of a one-off genetic experiment and separated at birth, are reunited in adulthood. They set out on a cross-country search for their unknown mother - fending off vicious loan sharks and a professional assassin along the way. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Danny DeVito, Kelly Preston and Chloe Webb

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis. A team of five from Holyhead take on the Tenable Tower as they attempt to win a £125,000 prize. Can they score a Perfect 10 to bring home the money?

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Thingamajig, Fox, Tree and Butterfly perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mikey North, Una Healy, Carl Froch and Alison Hammond pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the first episode, Stephen is joined by Coronation Street stars Jenni McAlpine, Antony Cotton and Sair Khan

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the beginning of this year's Battle Rounds. Solo singers and duos between the ages of seven and 14 perform to impress judges Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am enough to make them spin their famous red chairs around. There are 12 places in the semi-final up for grabs, bringing the talented performers who win them one step closer to a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Spy Who Loved Me (1977)
programme.desc: James Bond teams up with a KGB agent to recover missing British and Soviet nuclear submarines, before whoever stole them can use their warheads. Unfortunately, 007 was responsible for the death of his new ally's lover - and she is determined to make him pay before their mission is complete. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Barbara Bach, Curt Jurgens and Richard Kiel

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Holly Willoughby welcomes footballer Ashley Cole, Strictly Come Dancing 2016 winner Ore Oduba and comedian Kevin Bridges to the show. They join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh as well as regular panellist Romesh Ranganathan and scorekeeper Seann Walsh

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Dermot O'Leary, in which teams of three must take on challenges in miniature settings to win a cash prize of up to £150,000. The first episode's contestants are members of the England amputee football team

programme.title: Twins (1988)

They set out on a cross-country search for their unknown mother - fending off vicious loan sharks and a professional assassin along the way. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Danny DeVito, Kelly Preston and Chloe Webb

programme.title: Tenable

She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost

Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tom goes to school to explain himself to the headmaster. Viv follows Donna to find out where Kelly is staying. Rachel spreads the gossip about Eric getting engaged

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Glovers visit Dave's grave on what would have been his birthday. Frank and Chris are arrested for conspiracy to commit fraud. Eric's fiancee Dee arrives in the village

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fox tells the court how Vicky bribed him to change his statement, and her barrister paints a picture of Steve taking advantage and using her money

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is distraught over Steve's sentence and the fact Vicky walked free. The Duckworths return from Spain and Betty is relieved to hand the pub back to them

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house is made open to the public for a day to raise money for the hospital, an act that divides opinions among the family and the servants. Tensions between Violet and Cora come to a head as a decision is made about the hospital takeover, Mr Dawes has a proposition for Molesley, and Baxter is thrown into turmoil by an unexpected letter. Carson is not happy with Thomas, but all is not as it appears, while Mary and Edith hope to overcome their past and find new happiness with the men in their lives

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Maggie gets the all-clear from the doctor and decides she will return to Bude, forcing PC Penhale to take desperate measures to persuade her to stay in Portwenn. Ruth uncovers a raft of family heirlooms while clearing out a barn at the farm, and the mounting tension between Martin and Louisa peaks when she discovers he has booked a date for James Henry's christening without consulting her

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert struggles to come up with the money to repay loan sharks Alastair and Norman Tonken, who gave him £1,000 to clear his debts. Eleanor falls ill and Martin diagnoses a strangulated hernia, but when the ambulance goes to the wrong village, he has no alternative but to perform the emergency operation himself in his surgery. With Martin Clunes, Ian McNeice and Louise Jameson

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Dorothy becomes concerned her grand-daughter Taisie is being influenced by Rachel, while Janet is mortified when Gill berates her for inappropriate behaviour toward young sergeant Rob. The detective duo are sent to investigate the death of a man in a nursing home after his daughter complains about the level of care he had been receiving. Will they find any evidence of foul play? Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Danny Miller

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert, Angie and Ida's move north goes from bad to worse when they eventually arrive only to discover the furniture hasn't

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida finds her new home uncomfortable, until a special delivery arrives

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Police ask Sarah about the accident, and why she called 999 before the collision happened. In Roy's Rolls, Elaine introduces herself to Sally, and explains that she is Tim's mother. And Jenny sympathises with Abi over the twins' imminent departure for Australia

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally implores Tim to accept that Elaine could be telling the truth and Geoff could have fed him a pack of lies all his life. Elsewhere, Leanne is overwhelmed with grief, and when Carla lets slip that Gary's binned his threat of a rent increase, Maria's concerned

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff swears that Elaine is peddling lies, she's not Tim's Mum and Alya must have put her up to it in a bid to drive a wedge between them. Leanne urges Toyah to set the fostering wheels in motion as she deserves some happiness. And Adam seethes when Sarah fails to mention Gary's change of heart over the rent

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The body of Dr Stephen Black is found on an Oxford tour bus, and the detectives are led to sprawling Crevecoeur Hall, where Hathaway spent much of his childhood. As he investigates, he is drawn to Scarlett Mortmaigne, the glamorous daughter of the estate's owner, but when another corpse is discovered, Hathaway's refusal to believe the family is involved threatens to test his relationship with Lewis to breaking point. Guest starring Nathaniel Parker (The Inspector Lynley Mysteries), Juliet Aubrey (Primeval) and Stella Gonet (Holby City), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of prison officer Charlie Tyner, Brooks and Devlin discover he had been abusing his position and convicts in his charge. A suspect is brought to trial, but Steel and Phillips go in pursuit of a career criminal connected to Tyner and have to persuade their key witness to give evidence against a man she claims to love. Starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels along the Staffordshire and Worcestershire canal, widely regarded as one of the oldest and prettiest waterways in Britain. On his journey he marvels at ingenious 18th-century engineering, tackles the difficult skill of glassblowing and discovers a secret underground cold war bunker

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida and Angie brace themselves for a visit by Robert's 'other mother'. But they're not prepared for following events

programme.desc: Text-based information service
